_doc: Single source of truth for the quasar framework test matrix. Each case declares its driver inputs (design/config/oracle/device_sources/generate_devices) and its 'kind' (oracle|smoke), which decides how a result is judged. Cells are (case x backend x os x compiler); .CI/expand_matrix.py flattens them into a GitHub Actions strategy.matrix, and run_test_case.py --case reads the SAME file so a case is declared exactly once. The CI image of a cell is ghcr.io/<owner>/quasar-ci:<os>-<backend>[-<toolkit_version>]. Backend coverage is symmetric -- every case runs on both o6 and uasdk now that the backends are at feature parity (a short per-cell exclude would capture any genuine semantic delta; there are none today). PR/nightly tiers and the UASDK-version axis are layered on top.
_nightly_os_doc: OSes that mirror the REPRESENTATIVE case set (see representative_cases) at nightly tier -- the EL-major delta is platform-level, so a few broad-surface cases re-run there rather than the whole set. default_os carries the per-case/per-version tiers (PR + nightly). An entry is either a bare "alma10" (sweep every uasdk version) or {"os":"alma10","versions":[...]} to mirror only the listed uasdk versions on that EL. Explicit per-case extra_cells (e.g. default_design's PR alma10 smoke + clang) are preserved -- they are emitted first and win de-duplication.
_nightly_compilers_doc: Extra compiler(s) run over the FULL case set at nightly tier (compiler-portability coverage beyond the default gcc). NOT narrowed by representative_cases: a compiler can break on case-specific generated C++ (templates/headers), so every case is compiled under the alt compiler. clang on alma10/o6 -- uasdk static libs are gcc-built so clang is kept to o6 to avoid a cross-compiler-ABI variable.
_nightly_arches_doc: Extra CPU architectures run at nightly on native GitHub arm64 runners (ubuntu-24.04-arm, free for public repos) with arch-tagged images (e.g. quasar-ci:alma9-o6-arm64). 'versions' limits which uasdk toolkit versions get an arch image built; o6 + the default (PR-tier) uasdk version on alma9 is the representative aarch64 set (the user ships aarch64 SDK builds). representative_cases (if set) further restricts the case set -- arch is a platform-level delta. Same gcc/libstdc++ family as x86_64, so the framework/compat/uasak builds are portable.
_representative_cases_doc: Broad-surface cases re-run on the case-INDEPENDENT secondary axes (extended OS, alt arch, and non-default/nightly uasdk versions). Those deltas are platform/SDK-level, so the full per-case sweep stays on default_os + the default uasdk version and only this subset mirrors onto them. Chosen to exercise the distinct codegen/runtime surfaces (plain + async methods, cache + source variables). NOTE: nightly_compilers (clang) is deliberately NOT narrowed by this list -- a compiler can fail on case-specific generated code, so it keeps the full case set. Remove this key (or set []) to restore the full case set on every axis.
